title: Trimming Clips and Building a Rough Cut

video.note: Covers trimming clip edges, removing excess slate or dead time, choosing story moments, setting in and out points, adding selected ranges to a timeline, insert and append edits, trim mode, ripple trimming, split edits, and keyboard shortcut awareness.

learningTarget: I can trim clips, set in and out points, and build a rough cut that communicates a simple story without unnecessary dead space.

bellRinger.prompt: What makes a video clip feel too long, even if the shot itself is useful?

vocabulary.definition: To shorten or extend a clip by adjusting where it starts or ends.
The selected start of the part of a source clip that will be used.
The selected end of the part of a source clip that will be used.
An early version of an edit focused on order, timing, and story instead of polish.
An edit that places a clip and pushes later material down the timeline.
A trim that closes or opens timeline space so clips stay connected.
A cut that divides one clip into separate pieces.

slides.title: Lesson 04 - Trimming Clips and Building a Rough Cut

assignment.title: Rough Cut Story Sequence
evidenceRequired: Google Drive link to a screenshot of the rough cut timeline.
Google Drive or YouTube link to a short exported rough cut if teacher requests export.
Short reflection naming at least three tools or shortcuts used.

exitTicket: Name one trimming tool or shortcut you used today and explain what it changed in your rough cut.
